title: Tis Good, Lord, To Be Here

X: 84
T: Tis Good, Lord, To Be Here
%%combinevoices 1
C: Words: Joseph Armitage Robinson, 1888.
C: Music: 'Potsdam' adapted from Johann Sebastian Bach, 1750, by John Goss, 1854. Setting: John Goss, 1854.

w: Thy face and gar- ments, like the sun, Shine with un- bor- rowed light.

w: We hail Thy bo- dy glor- i- fied And our re- demp- tion see. 
w: We fain would hold the vi- sion bright And make this hill our home. 
w: But since Thou bidst us leave the mount, Come with us to the plain.

metadata.composer: 'Potsdam' adapted from Johann Sebastian Bach, 1750, by John Goss, 1854 John Goss, 1854
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2011 Revision.
c_lines: Music: 'Potsdam' adapted from Johann Sebastian Bach, 1750, by John Goss, 1854. Setting: John Goss, 1854.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2011 Revision.

1.raw_text: 'Tis good, Lord, to be here, Thy glory fills the night; Thy face and garments, like the sun, Shine with unborrowed light.

2.raw_text: 'Tis good, Lord, to be here, Thy beauty to behold Where Moses and Eli- jah stand, Thy messengers of old.

3.raw_text: Fulfiller of the past, Promise of things to be, We hail Thy body glori- fied And our redemption see.

4.raw_text: Before we taste of death, We see Thy Kingdom come; We fain would hold the vision bright And make this hill our home.

5.raw_text: 'Tis good, Lord, to be here. Yet we may not remain; But since Thou bidst us leave the mount, Come with us to the plain.
